silentHillTownfallStorySetting.title: Silent Hill Townfall Story and St. Amelia Setting
silentHillTownfallStorySetting.intro: Silent Hill Townfall follows Simon Ordell as he returns to St. Amelia, an isolated island town wrapped in fog, guilt, memory, and unstable analog signals.

items.title: Simon Ordell
items.description: Simon is called back to St. Amelia to put things right, and the town keeps pulling him toward personal damage he has not escaped.

items.title: St. Amelia
items.description: The island town feels abandoned but never empty, with streets, housing blocks, and waterfront spaces loaded with dread.

items.title: Scotland, 1996
items.description: The period setting sharpens the analog identity of the game through damp weather, old tech, and a grounded remote-town atmosphere.

items.title: Memory, Loss, and Consequence
items.description: Silent Hill Townfall builds story pressure through fractured recollection, grief, and the sense that every choice deepens the damage.

items.title: CRTV and Static
items.description: The CRTV feeds Simon unstable signals, story fragments, and warning signs that make the town feel alive even when nothing is speaking.

items.title: Narrative-Driven Puzzles
items.description: Puzzles are framed as part of Simon’s understanding of St. Amelia rather than separate detached brainteasers.

silentHillTownfallGameplayCombatCrtv.title: Silent Hill Townfall Gameplay Combat and CRTV
silentHillTownfallGameplayCombatCrtv.intro: Silent Hill Townfall plays entirely in first person, pushing Simon Ordell through tense exploration, stealth pressure, direct combat, narrative-driven puzzles, and CRTV signal reading.

items.title: Explore St. Amelia in first person
items.description: The first-person view keeps every corridor, doorway, and shoreline close to Simon, making clue hunting, environmental reading, and horror encounters feel immediate.

items.title: Use the CRTV as your main survival tool
items.description: The CRTV acts like a new version of the classic Silent Hill radio, translating unstable analog signals into warning signs, route clues, and story fragments.

items.title: Read threats before committing
items.description: Signal shifts, peeking, and careful staging help you judge enemy positions before stepping into a room that can collapse into panic.

items.title: Choose melee or firearms when combat is needed
items.description: Melee weapons and firearms are available, but combat is framed as a risky response for bad situations rather than the default solution.

items.title: Run, distract, hide, or fight
items.description: Some encounters reward evasion more than aggression, so survival depends on breaking line of sight, using the environment, and switching tactics quickly.

items.title: Solve puzzles that carry story meaning
items.description: Puzzles are woven into Simon's return to St. Amelia, using environmental details and story context to reveal what the town has been hiding.

silentHillTownfallBeginnerGuide.title: Silent Hill Townfall Beginner Guide
silentHillTownfallBeginnerGuide.intro: Treat Silent Hill Townfall as a survival horror game about attention, restraint, and clean decision-making. The safest first-run habit is to observe before you commit.

items.title: Raise the CRTV before entering danger
items.description: Check the CRTV around dark interiors, puzzle spaces, and enemy routes so you can read unstable signals before taking another step.

items.title: Peek before moving into open spaces
items.description: Use corners, cover, and sightlines carefully, because spotting danger first is usually more valuable than landing the first hit.

items.title: Hide or evade when the enemy is too dangerous
items.description: If an encounter starts to spiral, treat escape as the win condition and protect your route out instead of forcing a messy fight.

items.title: Fight only when it gives you a clear advantage
items.description: Weapons matter most when an enemy blocks progress, your escape route is gone, or the room clearly favors a fast decisive response.

items.title: Treat puzzles as part of the story
items.description: Read notes, props, signals, and room details as connected clues so puzzle solving feels like understanding St. Amelia instead of hunting random switches.

items.title: Explore carefully because actions matter
items.description: Slow exploration helps you catch optional details, preserve resources, and understand how your actions feed into Silent Hill Townfall's ending paths.

silentHillTownfallPCRequirementsLanguage.title: Silent Hill Townfall PC Requirements and Languages
silentHillTownfallPCRequirementsLanguage.intro: PC players need Windows 11 x64, DirectX 12, and 75 GB of storage. Minimum specs target 1080p 30fps on Low, while the recommended setup targets 4K 30fps on High with upscaling.

items.minimum: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 Super / AMD Radeon RX 6600
items.recommended: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 / AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT
High settings at 4K 30fps using DLSS, FSR, or TSR Balanced mode

items.minimum: English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Polish, Portuguese, Russian, Spanish (Spain), Spanish (Latin America), Chinese (Simplified), Chinese (Traditional)

silentHillTownfallDeveloperScreenBurn.title: Silent Hill Townfall Developer, No Code, and Screen Burn
silentHillTownfallDeveloperScreenBurn.intro: Silent Hill Townfall is developed by Screen Burn, the Glasgow studio formerly known as No Code. Its earlier work on Stories Untold and Observation helps explain the project's analog interfaces, dense atmosphere, and narrative-first tension.

items.question: Who is developing Silent Hill Townfall?
items.answer: Silent Hill Townfall is developed by Screen Burn, an independent game development studio based in Glasgow, Scotland.

items.question: Was the Silent Hill Townfall studio previously called No Code?
items.answer: Yes. Screen Burn was founded in 2015 under the original name No Code before later becoming Screen Burn Interactive.

items.question: What games did the Silent Hill Townfall studio make before this project?
items.answer: The studio is known for Stories Untold and Observation, both of which helped define its atmospheric storytelling style.

items.question: What is the creative style behind Silent Hill Townfall?
items.answer: The team focuses on atmosphere, story, polished presentation, detailed sound design, and retro interface ideas that fit psychological horror well.

items.question: What is KONAMI’s role in Silent Hill Townfall?
items.answer: KONAMI owns the Silent Hill franchise, co-publishes Silent Hill Townfall, and announced the release date and platform rollout.

items.question: What is Annapurna Interactive’s role in Silent Hill Townfall?
items.answer: Annapurna Interactive co-publishes Silent Hill Townfall and supports its storefront presence across PlayStation, Steam, and Epic Games Store.

items.question: How does No Code’s past work connect to Silent Hill Townfall?
items.answer: The team has long worked with retro interfaces, VHS-style presentation, and story-led design, which fits the CRTV mechanic and first-person tension of Townfall.
